nodes.itemHTML: • archivenowstars263 - A Python library to push web resources into on-demand web archives. (Stable)
• Chroniclerstars68 - Web browser with record and replay functionality. (In Development)
• Crawl - A simple web crawler in Golang. (Stable)
• crocoitestars37 - Crawl websites using headless Google Chrome/Chromium and save resources, static DOM snapshot and page screenshots to WARC files. (In Development)
• F(b)arcstars72 - A commandline tool and Python library for archiving data from Facebook using the Graph API. (Stable)
• freeze-drystars157 - JavaScript library to turn page into static, self-contained HTML document; useful for browser extensions. (In Development)
• grab-sitestars699 - The archivist's web crawler: WARC output, dashboard for all crawls, dynamic ignore patterns. (Stable)

• Heritrix - An open source, extensible, web-scale, archival quality web crawler. (Stable)
    • Heritrix Walkthroughstars8 (In Development)
• html2warcstars7 - A simple script to convert offline data into a single WARC file. (Stable)
• HTTrack - An open source website copying utility. (Stable)
• monolithstars4.2k - CLI tool to save a web page as a single HTML file. (Stable)
• SingleFilestars3.3k - Browser extension for Firefox/Chrome and CLI tool to save a faithful copy of a complete page as a single HTML file. (Stable)
• SiteStory - A transactional archive that selectively captures and stores transactions that take place between a web client (browser) and a web server. (Stable)
• Social Feed Manager - Open source software that enables users to create social media collections from Twitter, Tumblr, Flickr, and Sina Weibo public APIs. (Stable)
• Squidwarcstars128 - An open source, high-fidelity, page interacting archival crawler that uses Chrome or Chrome Headless directly. (In Development)
• StormCrawler - A collection of resources for building low-latency, scalable web crawlers on Apache Storm. (Stable)
• twarcstars1k - A command line tool and Python library for archiving Twitter JSON data. (Stable)
• WARCreate - A Google Chrome extension for archiving an individual webpage or website to a WARC file. (Stable)
• Warcworkerstars40 - An open source, dockerized, queued, high fidelity web archiver based on Squidwarc with a simple web GUI. (Stable)
• Web2Warcstars19 - An easy-to-use and highly customizable crawler that enables anyone to create their own little Web archives (WARC/CDX). (Stable)
• Webrecorder - Create high-fidelity, interactive recordings of any web site you browse. (Stable)
• Wget - An open source file retrieval utility that of version 1.14 supports writing warcs. (Stable)
• Wget-luastars20 - Wget with Lua extension. (Stable)
• Wpullstars421 - A Wget-compatible (or remake/clone/replacement/alternative) web downloader and crawler. (Stable)
• Tempas v1 - Temporal web archive search based on Delicious tags. (Stable)

• Tempas v2 - Temporal web archive search based on links and anchor texts extracted from the German web from 1996 to 2013 (results are not limited to German pages, e.g., Obama@2005-2009 in Tempas). (Stable)

• webarchive-discoverystars89 - WARC and ARC full-text indexing and discovery tools, with a number of associated tools capable of using the index shown below. (Stable)
    • Shinestars37 - A prototype web archives exploration UI, developed with researchers as part of the Big UK Domain Data for the Arts and Humanities project. (Stable)
    • SolrWaybackstars46 - A backend Java and frontend VUE JS project with freetext search and a build in playback engine. Require Warc files has been index with the Warc-Indexer. The web application also has a wide range of data visualization tools and data export tools that can be used on the whole webarchive. SolrWayback 4 Bundle release contains all the software and dependencies in an out-of-the box solution that is easy to install.
    • Warclightstars38 - A Project Blacklight based Rails engine that supports the discovery of web archives held in the WARC and ARC formats. (In Development)
    • Waspstars17 - A fully functional prototype of a personal web archive and search system. (In Development)
    • Other possible options for builting a front-end are listed on in the webarchive-discovery wiki, here.
• httpreserve.info - Service to return the status of a web page or save it to the Internet Archive. Returns JSON via browser or command line via CURL using GET (Golang Package). (Stable)

• HTTPreserve Workbenchstars2 - Tool and API to describe the status of a web page encoded in a simple JSON output describing current status, and earliest and latest links on wayback.org. Save a web page to the Internet Archive. Audit lists of URIs and output a CSV with the data described above (Golang). (In Development)
• MementoMapstars3 - A Tool to Summarize Web Archive Holdings (Python). (In Development)
• MemGatorstars38 - A Memento Aggregator CLI and Server (Golang). (Stable)
• node-cdxjstars0 - CDXJ file parser (Node.js). (Stable)
• OutbackCDXstars15 - RocksDB-based capture index (CDX) server supporting incremental updates and compression. Can be used as backend for OpenWayback, PyWb and Heritrix. (Stable)
• py-wasapi-clientstars12 - Command line application to download crawls from WASAPI (Python). (Stable)
• tikalinkextractstars4 - Extract hyperlinks as a seed for web archiving from folders of document types that can be parsed by Apache Tika (Golang, Apache Tika Server). (In Development)
• wasapi-downloaderstars4 - Java command line application to download crawls from WASAPI. (Stable)
• WarcPartitionerstars0 - Partition (W)ARC Files by MIME Type and Year. (Stable)
• wikiteamstars410 - Tools for downloading and preserving wikis. (Stable)
• HadoopConcatGzstars9 - A Splitable Hadoop InputFormat for Concatenated GZIP Files (and *.warc.gz). (Stable)
• Jwat - Libraries and tools for reading/writing/validating WARC/ARC/GZIP files (Java). (Stable)
• node-warcstars54 - Parse WARC files or create WARC files using either Electron or chrome-remote-interfacestars3.4k (Node.js). (Stable)
• Warcatstars94 - Tool and library for handling Web ARChive (WARC) files (Python). (Stable)
• ArchiveSparkstars113 - An Apache Spark framework (not only) for Web Archives that enables easy data processing, extraction as well as derivation. (Stable)

• Archives Unleashed Cloud - Archives Unleashed Cloud (AUK) is an web interface for analysing web archives. Currently, it can sync with Archive-It collections and extract hyperlink networks, full text, and other information from your collections. (Stable)
• Archives Unleashed Notebooksstars6 - Notebooks for working with web archives with the Archives Unleashed Toolkit, and derivatives generated by the Archives Unleashed Toolkit. (Stable)
• Archives Unleashed Toolkitstars105 - Archives Unleashed Toolkit (AUT) is an open-source platform for analyzing web archives with Apache Spark. (Stable)
• Tweet Archvies Unleashed Toolkitstars6 - An open-source toolkit for analyzing line-oriented JSON Twitter archives with Apache Spark. (In Development)
• Crowd Inspect - Free tool for Windows systems aimed to alert you to the presence of malware that may be communicating over the network.
• Wazuh - Open source, multiplatform agent-based security monitoring based on a fork of OSSEC HIDS.
• ChopShopstars450 - Framework to aid analysts in the creation and execution of pynids-based decoders and detectors of APT tradecraft.
• Maltrailstars4k - Malicious network traffic detection system.
• Molochstars4.8k - Augments your current security infrastructure to store and index network traffic in standard PCAP format, providing fast, indexed access.
• OwlH - Helps manage network IDS at scale by visualizing Suricata, Zeek, and Moloch life cycles.
• Snort - Widely-deployed, Free Software IPS capable of real-time packet analysis, traffic logging, and custom rule-based triggers.
• SpoofSpotterstars44 - Catch spoofed NetBIOS Name Service (NBNS) responses and alert to an email or log file.
• Suricata - Free, cross-platform, IDS/IPS with on- and off-line analysis modes and deep packet inspection capabilities that is also scriptable with Lua.
• Wireshark - Free and open-source packet analyzer useful for network troubleshooting or forensic netflow analysis.

• netsniff-ng - Free and fast GNU/Linux networking toolkit with numerous utilities such as a connection tracking tool (flowtop), traffic generator (trafgen), and autonomous system (AS) trace route utility (astraceroute).
• CimSweepstars565 - Suite of CIM/WMI-based tools enabling remote incident response and hunting operations across all versions of Windows.
• DeepBlueCLIstars1k - PowerShell module for hunt teaming via Windows Event logs.

• GRR Rapid Responsestars3.8k - Incident response framework focused on remote live forensics consisting of a Python agent installed on assets and Python-based server infrastructure enabling analysts to quickly triage attacks and perform analysis remotely.

• Hunting ELK (HELK)stars2.9k - All-in-one Free Software threat hunting stack based on Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kafka, and Kibana with various built-in integrations for analytics including Jupyter Notebook.
• MozDefstars2.1k - Automate the security incident handling process and facilitate the real-time activities of incident handlers.
• PSHuntstars211 - PowerShell module designed to scan remote endpoints for indicators of compromise or survey them for more comprehensive information related to state of those systems.
• PSReconstars419 - PSHunt-like tool for analyzing remote Windows systems that also produces a self-contained HTML report of its findings.
• PowerForensicsstars1.1k - All in one PowerShell-based platform to perform live hard disk forensic analysis.
• rastrea2rstars172 - Multi-platform tool for triaging suspected IOCs on many endpoints simultaneously and that integrates with antivirus consoles.
• Redline - Freeware endpoint auditing and analysis tool that provides host-based investigative capabilities, offered by FireEye, Inc.
• Has Changed Pathstars117 - Conditionally run actions based on changed paths.
• Omnispeakstars232 - Reimplementation of Commander Keen in Goodbye Galaxy.
• SoftMaker Office A complete office suite that aims for full compatibility with Microsoft Office documents
• wateringcan.work - Christian Tech Jobs
• Blazor StateHasChanged: Hot take on hot questions - March 20, 2020 - Blazor StateHasChanged: Hot take on hot questions, by Ed Charbeneau.

• Create a Blazor Server App and Add Syncfusion Blazor Components - March 24, 2020 - Learn to create a new Blazor Server App and add Blazor Components of Syncfusion to it using Visual Studio 2019 and Visual Studio Code.

• Reference Storefrontstars24 - Sitecore Reference Storefront.

• Sitecore Habitatstars408 - Habitat is an example Sitecore solution built on the Helix architecture principles. No longer being updated or maintained, and is being replaced with Sitecore Helix Examples.
• SwiftyMenustars372 - A Simple and Elegant DropDown menu for iOS 🔥💥
